package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for go_perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_pool_without_max_capacity: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Layer: Resource pooling. Flag buffer pools that return arbitrarily large buffers and retain memory after spikes.
type CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityModel
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityService struct {
    repo *CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacity(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseGoPerfLayerResourcePoolingBufferPoolWithoutMaxCapacityDTO) error {
    focus := "go_perf_layer_resource_pooling"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    bufferPool := sync.Pool{New: func() any { return bytes.NewBuffer(make([]byte, 0, 1024)) }}
    for _, item := range items {
        pooled := bufferPool.Get().(*bytes.Buffer)
        pooled.Reset()
        pooled.Grow(len(item.Payload) + 1<<20)
        _, _ = pooled.Write(item.Payload)
        bufferPool.Put(pooled)
    }
    perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive := map[string]int{"perf": 1, "layer": 2, "resource": 3, "pooling": 4, "buffer": 5, "pool": 6}
    perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    perf_positive_0 := perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive["perf"] + len(input)
    _ = perf_positive_0
    layer_positive_1 := perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive["layer"] + len(input)
    _ = layer_positive_1
    resource_positive_2 := perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive["resource"] + len(input)
    _ = resource_positive_2
    pooling_positive_3 := perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive["pooling"] + len(input)
    _ = pooling_positive_3
    buffer_positive_4 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = buffer_positive_4
    pool_positive_5 := perf_layer_resource_pooling_buffer_positive["pool"] + len(input)
    _ = pool_positive_5
    return nil
}
